root_cause.text: The reactive `HiddenHttpMethodFilter` previously allowed any arbitrary HTTP method (such as TRACE) to be simulated via a request parameter without restriction, potentially allowing attackers to bypass method-based access controls or perform unwanted actions.
root_cause.evidence: The patch introduces an `ALLOWED_METHODS` list (`PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) and adds a condition `if (ALLOWED_METHODS.contains(httpMethod))` in `mapExchange` to prevent mutating the request to unauthorized HTTP methods like `TRACE`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The reactive `HiddenHttpMethodFilter` previously allowed any arbitrary HTTP method (such as TRACE) to be simulated via a request parameter without restriction, potentially allowing attackers to bypass method-based access controls or perform unwanted actions.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch introduces an `ALLOWED_METHODS` list (`PUT`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`) and adds a condition `if (ALLOWED_METHODS.contains(httpMethod))` in `mapExchange` to prevent mutating the request to unauthorized HTTP methods like `TRACE`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update the copyright year to 2018 in the file header.
intent_record.what: The copyright year in the header comment was updated from 2017 to 2018.
intent_record.how: This change has no functional impact on the execution or behavior of the program.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add necessary utility and collection imports.
intent_record.what: Added imports for Arrays, Collections, and List.
intent_record.how: Makes standard Java collection classes available for use in the file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a constant list of allowed HTTP methods to restrict method overriding.
intent_record.what: Adds a static, unmodifiable list of allowed HTTP methods (PUT, DELETE, PATCH).
intent_record.how: Provides a predefined set of HTTP methods that can be safely processed, likely to restrict method overriding.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a guard to only allow mutating the exchange with permitted HTTP methods.
intent_record.what: Added a condition to check if the parsed HTTP method is in the ALLOWED_METHODS list before mutating the request exchange.
intent_record.how: Prevents the exchange from being mutated with disallowed HTTP methods, returning the unmodified exchange if the method is not permitted.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update the copyright year to 2018 in the license header.
intent_record.what: The copyright year in the header comment was updated from 2017 to 2018.
intent_record.how: This change has no functional impact on the program behavior as it only modifies a comment.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a test to verify HiddenHttpMethodFilter behavior with disallowed HTTP methods.
intent_record.what: Added a new test method filterWithParameterMethodNotAllowed.
intent_record.how: Verifies that providing an unsupported method like TRACE results in the original POST method being retained.

grouping_result.intent: Update the copyright year to 2018 in the file header.
Add necessary utility and collection imports.
Add a guard to only allow mutating the exchange with permitted HTTP methods.
Update the copyright year to 2018 in the license header.
Add a test to verify HiddenHttpMethodFilter behavior with disallowed HTTP methods.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add a guard and permitted HTTP methods list to restrict exchange mutations to allowed methods.

relevance_review.reason: Updating the copyright year is a purely administrative change and does not address the vulnerability regarding the restriction of simulated HTTP methods.
The group only adds utility and collection imports, which do not directly address or implement the logic to restrict simulated HTTP methods.

The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding a list of permitted HTTP methods and a guard to restrict method overriding, which prevents attackers from simulating arbitrary or restricted methods.
Updating the copyright year in the license header is an administrative change and has no relation to the vulnerability involving HTTP method filtering.
The group adds tests specifically verifying the new behavior of HiddenHttpMethodFilter with disallowed methods, which directly addresses the vulnerability's root cause.

candidates.rationale: This line blindly mutates the exchange with the parsed HTTP method without checking if the HTTP method is allowed to be simulated (e.g., TRACE), causing the vulnerability.
This resolves the method from the user input which is then used to override the request method without any validation of permitted methods.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement blindly mutates the HTTP exchange with the user-supplied HTTP method without verifying if the method is permitted, leading to the vulnerability.
This statement resolves the user-supplied HTTP method from the request parameter, which is subsequently used without validation.

index 1fdeac1e55..399e6ec3cc 100644

bic_trace_step.reason: The filter method allows any HTTP method to be set via the form parameter, as seen in `HttpMethod.resolve(methodParamValue.toUpperCase(Locale.ENGLISH))`. There is no restriction to specific safe methods (like PUT, DELETE, PATCH), meaning the vulnerability still exists.

index de0f19a9df..1fdeac1e55 100644

This allows any HTTP method, including TRACE, to be simulated via the hidden parameter, which matches the vulnerability root cause.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit e4be7131ca74c23275837684ce63d839469a0dda
Author: Greg Turnquist <gturnquist@pivotal.io>
Date:   Mon Feb 6 13:46:55 2017 -0600

    Implement reactive HiddenHttpMethodFilter
    
    Provides the ability to embed an HTTP verb inside a hidden form field
    (like Thymeleaf's th:method operator).
    
    Issue: SPR-15206

diff --git a/spring-web/src/main/java/org/springframework/web/filter/reactive/HiddenHttpMethodFilter.java b/spring-web/src/main/java/org/springframework/web/filter/reactiv
bic_trace_step.reason: The `convertedRequest` method takes the HTTP method from the `methodParam`, converts it to uppercase, and uses `HttpMethod.resolve(upperMethod)` to set the new method without any restrictions on allowed HTTP methods. This introduces the vulnerability where any arbitrary HTTP method can be simulated.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit a99fe3eda4af496cd6e2b90b7f9988a76fe6695e
Author: Rossen Stoyanchev <rstoyanchev@pivotal.io>
Date:   Thu Mar 16 15:00:47 2017 -0400

    Polish HttpHiddenMethodFilter

diff --git a/spring-web/src/main/java/org/springframework/web/filter/reactive/HiddenHttpMethodFilter.java b/spring-web/src/main/java/org/springframework/web/filter/reactive/HiddenHttpMethodFilter.java
index 1fdeac1e55..399e6ec3cc 100644
--- a/spring-web/src/main/java/org/springframework/web/filter/reactive/HiddenHttpMethodFi
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `mapExchange` (and its predecessor `convertedRequest`) resolves the HTTP method from the request parameter using `HttpMethod.resolve(...)` without any validation against a list of allowed methods. This allows any arbitrary method to be simulated, matching the root cause of the vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit e4be7131ca74c23275837684ce63d839469a0dda
Author: Greg Turnquist <gturnquist@pivotal.io>
Date:   Mon Feb 6 13:46:55 2017 -0600

    Implement reactive HiddenHttpMethodFilter
    
    Provides the ability to embed an HTTP verb inside a hidden form field
    (like Thymeleaf's th:method operator).
    
    Issue: SPR-15206

diff --git a/spring-web/src/main/java/org/springframework/web/filter/reactive/HiddenHttpMethodFilter.java b/spring-web/src/main/java/org/springframework/web/filter/reactiv
bic_trace_step.reason: The `HiddenHttpMethodFilter` implementation in this commit maps the hidden method parameter to any HTTP method using `HttpMethod.resolve(upperMethod)` without restricting it to standard allowed methods (e.g., PUT, DELETE, PATCH). This allows attackers to simulate restricted methods like TRACE, introducing the vulnerability.
